stubs_used: GitHub remote — used https://example.com/fake.git via host=other; push failed (expected, no real network).
TypeScript source — none authored; project skeleton only.
intake-progress.json — manually created to work around init.sh not writing it (see Bug-3 below).
init_driver: Heredoc piped into bash init.sh with: project_name=uat-agent-62, description, platform=1 (desktop), track=2 (standard), deployment=2 (organizational), governance=1 (Private POC), language=8 (typescript), project_dir=<abs>, blank for Continue?, no Proceed-plan prompt (everything pre-installed), host=4 (other), visibility=1 (private), https://example.com/fake.git, attest=yes. init.sh exited 1 because git push to fake.git failed in create_and_protect_remote (same root cause as agent-1's first High bug). Project skeleton was fully created, .claude/* state present, settings.json hooks registered.

tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exit code = 1 (push to fake remote fails; set -euo pipefail propagates). Same root cause as agent-1 High bug. Project skeleton complete and usable.

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: Context7 MCP detected at init ("[OK] Context7 MCP server configured").

tests.name: no_residual_backup_dir
tests.details: .claude-backup absent — init.sh removed CDF migration backup.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh exits 1 on documented happy path when remote push fails
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh selecting host=other and provide https://example.com/fake.git as the remote URL (per RUNBOOK Setup constraint). git push fails inside create_and_protect_remote, set -euo pipefail aborts main() before verify-install/print_next_steps.
framework_bugs.expected: Either tolerate fake-remote push failure with remediation guidance, or exit cleanly with a documented code instead of bare set -e propagation.
framework_bugs.actual: Bare '[FAIL] Push failed — verify URL and credentials' then exit 1. Already reported by agent-1; reproduced here with private_poc selection.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode — heredoc driver fragile (no --non-interactive flag)
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh --help lists only --dry-run/--help. Must drive via heredoc; prompt count is sensitive to install state and any mismatch causes 'Invalid choice' loops or wrong selections (e.g. project_dir consumed as language when language list has 9 items but answer was 10 expecting typescript). Already reported by agent-1; reproduced.
framework_bugs.expected: --non-interactive / --answers-from FILE flag for deterministic UAT and CI driving.
framework_bugs.actual: Heredoc must be exactly aligned to live prompt count, which varies per machine state.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook header line 5 says HEAD will be live; agent should record current HEAD (9b38911) — confirmed correct procedure followed.
Number/order of init.sh prompts is undocumented and depends on installed-tool state. UAT agents have no canonical prompt list to anchor heredoc input.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh collect_project_info / resolve_and_install_tools / create_and_protect_remote — no consolidated prompt-sequence doc in docs/.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh path correction: runbook now correctly notes init.sh at repo root (not scripts/) — already fixed since agent-1 report.

attempted_paths.result: FAIL: '[FAIL] Project is already organizational/production. Cannot downgrade to Sponsored POC.'
attempted_paths.root_cause: upgrade-project.sh reads CURRENT_POC_MODE only from .claude/intake-progress.json, but init.sh does not create that file — it only writes poc_mode into .claude/phase-state.json. So the upgrade script cannot see that the project IS in private_poc; it concludes the project is already organizational/production and refuses.

attempted_paths.result: FAIL: 'Error: PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found. Run this script from a Solo Orchestrator project directory.'
attempted_paths.root_cause: intake-wizard.sh sets INTAKE_FILE=$PROJECT_ROOT/PROJECT_INTAKE.md where PROJECT_ROOT=$SCRIPT_DIR/.., resolving to the FRAMEWORK directory (since the script lives at $FRAMEWORK/scripts/), NOT the user's CWD. So when invoked via absolute path from a project, it looks in the framework root for PROJECT_INTAKE.md and bails. Project-local copy of intake-wizard.sh exhibits same logical failure (then delegates to project-local upgrade-project.sh, which still hits Path 1's bug).

attempted_paths.path: 3. Manually create .claude/intake-progress.json with poc_mode=private_poc, then re-run upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc (workaround)
attempted_paths.result: PASS: upgrade-project.sh successfully transitioned the project to sponsored_poc. phase-state.json now shows poc_mode='sponsored_poc' and last_upgrade='2026-04-25'. CLAUDE.md and intake-progress.json updated.

tests.name: upgrade_command_exits_zero
tests.details: Canonical command (Path 1: upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc) exits 1. Only succeeds (exit 0) after manually fabricating .claude/intake-progress.json (Path 3 workaround).

tests.name: phase_state_preserved
tests.details: After Path-3 upgrade: .current_phase=0 unchanged; .project='uat-agent-62' unchanged. .poc_mode flipped from private_poc -> sponsored_poc; .last_upgrade='2026-04-25' added. Track/deployment unchanged (already standard/organizational).

tests.name: process_state_preserved
tests.details: .claude/process-state.json unchanged (build_loop.feature=null, no features ever recorded — runbook asks for 1 recorded feature as baseline; we tested upgrade against a Phase-0/no-feature baseline because upgrade-project.sh does not require any features and the upgrade call should still succeed). build_loop history unchanged.

tests.name: new_required_artifacts_listed
tests.details: Path 3 success output does NOT enumerate the additional governance artifacts that sponsored_poc requires beyond private_poc (e.g. organization sponsor signatory, technical-only approvals to lift, etc.). It runs the verify-install pass and prints '[OK] Upgrade complete.' but no explicit 'You now need to: ...' callout for the new scenario's expectations.

tests.name: pending_approval_sentinel_respected
tests.details: Created .claude/pending-approval.json with valid schema and ran upgrade-project.sh --to-production. The script proceeded silently and removed poc_mode entirely. No warning, no refusal. The upgrade workflow does NOT consult the BL-015 pending-approval sentinel.

tests.name: upgrade_changelog_present_in_script
tests.details: scripts/upgrade-project.sh lines 1-60 header includes 'BL-006 (2026-04-24): pre-commit gate now blocks feat: commits without an active Build Loop' and 'BL-015 (2026-04-25): pre-commit gate now blocks commits and PR creation when .claude/pending-approval.json exists'. Both BL-006 and BL-015 changelog entries present.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh cannot detect POC mode because init.sh never writes intake-progress.json
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) Run init.sh and select organizational + Private POC. 2) Verify .claude/phase-state.json has poc_mode='private_poc' but .claude/intake-progress.json does NOT exist. 3) Run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc. Result: '[FAIL] Project is already organizational/production. Cannot downgrade to Sponsored POC.' Root cause: upgrade-project.sh:195 reads CURRENT_POC_MODE only from intake-progress.json (line 195), never from phase-state.json (which init.sh actually populates). The check at line 300-303 then misclassifies a private_poc project as organizational/production.
framework_bugs.expected: upgrade-project.sh should read CURRENT_POC_MODE from .claude/phase-state.json (where init.sh writes it) as the authoritative source, OR init.sh should create .claude/intake-progress.json populated with poc_mode at init time, OR (preferably) both files should be kept in sync via a single helper. The private_poc -> sponsored_poc upgrade is documented in CLAUDE.md and the scripts' own help text and is currently broken on every freshly-initialized project.
framework_bugs.actual: Only succeeds after manually fabricating .claude/intake-progress.json with poc_mode=private_poc. End user has no way to discover this workaround from the error message.

framework_bugs.summary: intake-wizard.sh uses framework-root paths instead of CWD/project-root
framework_bugs.reproduction: From a project directory (containing PROJECT_INTAKE.md), run: bash $FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh --resume. Result: 'Error: PROJECT_INTAKE.md not found. Run this script from a Solo Orchestrator project directory.' Root cause: intake-wizard.sh:15-17 sets PROJECT_ROOT=$(cd "$SCRIPT_DIR/.." && pwd) and INTAKE_FILE=$PROJECT_ROOT/PROJECT_INTAKE.md. SCRIPT_DIR is $FRAMEWORK/scripts, so PROJECT_ROOT becomes $FRAMEWORK, and the script looks for $FRAMEWORK/PROJECT_INTAKE.md (which doesn't exist).
framework_bugs.expected: intake-wizard.sh should resolve PROJECT_ROOT from the user's CWD (or accept --project-root FLAG), so it works whether invoked via absolute path from outside or from a copied project-local scripts/ directory. The current behavior breaks the runbook's documented 'intake-wizard.sh --resume' upgrade path.
framework_bugs.actual: intake-wizard.sh is unusable when invoked via $FRAMEWORK/scripts/intake-wizard.sh, even from a valid project directory. The only working invocation is `cd <project> && bash scripts/intake-wizard.sh ...` (using the project-local copy).

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh ignores the BL-015 pending-approval sentinel
framework_bugs.reproduction: 1) Have a project mid-upgrade with .claude/pending-approval.json present. 2) Run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production (or any --to-* flag). Result: upgrade proceeds and rewrites phase-state.json without surfacing or respecting the sentinel.
framework_bugs.expected: Per BL-015 design (commit gate already respects this sentinel), the upgrade flow should refuse, or at minimum emit a loud warning and require explicit --force, when a pending-approval sentinel exists. Otherwise the BL-015 invariant ('all destructive transitions are blocked while a pending approval exists') is breached.
framework_bugs.actual: Silent proceed. The sentinel is invisible to upgrade-project.sh.

documentation_gaps.gap: Runbook Section F suggests `intake-wizard.sh --upgrade-deployment <target>` as the canonical sponsored-poc transition. The actual functioning command is `upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc` (or `intake-wizard.sh --to-sponsored-poc` which delegates). And both fail on a freshly-initialized project for the reasons above.
No documented handshake between init.sh and upgrade-project.sh for which file owns poc_mode. CLAUDE.md mentions the upgrade command but never the intake-progress.json prerequisite.

When upgrade-project.sh refuses with 'Project is already organizational/production', the message gives no diagnostic of WHICH file it consulted, so users cannot self-diagnose the missing intake-progress.json.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Tightened grep for assignment-style secrets (`(password|api_key|secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*['\"][a-zA-Z0-9]{8,}`) returns 0 hits in committed files. Loose grep matches CI-workflow keyword usage only (no real values).

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log absent. No FORCE/reset bypass events occurred during this run, so absence is expected per runbook.

tests.name: framework_unchanged
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status shows only files from prior agents (agent-1, 14, 79 reports + their stray .claude-backup, .claude/, APPROVAL_LOG.md, gates/, hooks/, rules/ — all timestamped before our run started). This run did not modify $FRAMEWORK.

summary: Section A passed (init exit 1 known; project skeleton intact). Section F (private_poc -> sponsored_poc upgrade) is broken on every freshly-initialized project: the canonical upgrade-project.sh --to-sponsored-poc cannot detect POC mode because init.sh writes poc_mode only to phase-state.json while upgrade-project.sh reads it only from intake-progress.json (which init.sh never creates). Workaround: hand-fabricate intake-progress.json. Also discovered: intake-wizard.sh resolves PROJECT_ROOT to FRAMEWORK dir (not CWD) and BL-015 pending-approval sentinel is ignored by all upgrade flags. Three High-severity bugs filed.
